root_cause: User requested detailed trace of first-round Observer execution at fps=0.5/1.0
description: Created comprehensive markdown documenting the full code path when Observer performs a uniform global scan on a 1660s video: time range computation, ffmpeg re-encode params (480p/220k/6fps/no-audio), FPS adjustment from 1.0 to 0.31 due to max_frame_low=512, Blob/Part wrapping, prompt construction, API call structure, and response parsing into Evidence. Includes real Q1 test data showing the two clips generated and the low-FPS guard triggering.
verification: File created at repo root, content verified
recovery_notes: Pure documentation addition, no code changes

root_cause: Need an OpenAI-compatible client adapter so that Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct can be used as an alternative backend to GeminiClient in the AthenaQA (AVP) framework.
before: Only GeminiClient existed; no qwen_client.py file.
after: New avp/qwen_client.py (1160 lines) providing QwenClient class that mirrors GeminiClient interface: plan(), infer_on_video(), synthesize_final_answer(), create_video_part(), plus _OpenAICompatProxy for Reflector direct access pattern, _extract_frames for OpenCV frame extraction, and create_client() factory function.
why: Enables swapping Gemini for Qwen via cfg.backend="qwen" without changing Controller/Observer/Reflector/eval_dataset code.
verification: File parses without syntax errors; all required attributes and methods verified present on QwenClient instance; imports resolve correctly.
recovery_notes: File is new; deletion restores prior state.

root_cause: Need Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct backend support (方案B) to replace Gemini for native video understanding with MRoPE temporal encoding
symptom: Framework only supported Gemini 2.5 Pro backend; no open-source model option available
modified: avp/config.py (added 7 Qwen fields, 3 env overrides, 3 helper methods)
avp/eval_dataset.py (replaced GeminiClient instantiation with create_client factory)
avp/config.qwen.example.json (NEW, example config for Qwen backend)

changes_detail.avp/qwen_client.py: Complete QwenClient adapter: _OpenAICompatProxy (solves Reflector direct access gap), plan() with _apply_temporal_plan_guards, infer_on_video() with 3 video paths (region+multi, region+single, uniform) and canonical timebase normalization, synthesize_final_answer() with normalize_final_answer_output, create_video_part() with FPS clamping and 3 video modes (video/frames/auto), create_client() factory function
changes_detail.avp/config.py: Added backend, qwen_api_key, qwen_base_url, qwen_model, qwen_plan_model, qwen_execute_model, qwen_video_mode fields + AVP_BACKEND/QWEN_API_KEY/QWEN_BASE_URL env overrides + get_qwen_plan_model/get_qwen_execute_model/get_qwen_base_url helpers
changes_detail.avp/eval_dataset.py: Import create_client from qwen_client; replaced 15-line GeminiClient construction with 3-line create_client(cfg) call; preserved per-sample location override for Gemini backend
changes_detail.requirements.txt: Added openai>=1.0.0 dependency for OpenAI-compatible API calls

verification: All imports verified, factory pattern tested for both gemini and qwen backends, _ensure_v1 URL handling validated, all 7 required attributes confirmed present, Python syntax check passed on all files
recovery_notes: To revert: remove avp/qwen_client.py and avp/config.qwen.example.json, revert avp/eval_dataset.py and avp/config.py and requirements.txt to git HEAD. Config backend=gemini (default) preserves all existing Gemini behavior unchanged.
timeline_consistency: media_inputs dict with clip_time_base/absolute_start_sec/absolute_end_sec is constructed identically to GeminiClient; _normalize_key_evidence_to_canonical_timebase() and round_intervals_full_seconds() called at same points; ensures multi-agent (Observer->Reflector) timeline consistency

root_cause: User requested detailed agent interaction report from basketball test run
symptom: No detailed report existed documenting the Qwen backend test results
before: No test report; config.qwen.example.json used video_url mode (non-functional on yunwu.ai)
after: Detailed report (qwen_basketball_test_report.md) documents all 4 agents interactions, timeline, performance, and error analysis; config example updated to frames mode
why: Document test findings for the user, and fix example config to reflect the only working video mode on yunwu.ai proxy
verification: Report data cross-checked against role_traces.jsonl, evidence.json, final_answer.json, history.jsonl, conversation_history.json
recovery_notes: Report is append-only documentation. Config example change is cosmetic (default was already frames in code).

root_cause: User requested Gemini test on same basketball dataset for comparison with Qwen
before: No Gemini test results existed; no compressed video for yunwu.ai proxy compatibility
after: Gemini test completed: 2次三分 (correct), 164s total, 100% accuracy. Compressed video (78MB) created for proxy compatibility. Detailed comparison report written.
why: Compare Gemini native video understanding vs Qwen frames-only mode on identical task
verification: Gemini answered correctly (2次), Qwen answered incorrectly (3次). Gemini identified players by name, distinguished layup from 3pt shots.
recovery_notes: Compressed video at dataset/.../compressed/ is needed for Gemini via yunwu.ai. Original 496MB clips cause request_too_large errors.
